The friendship of wicked people is more dangerous than their hatred; for no one can defeat God’s people if they are not overcome by their own desires; nor can any magic harm them, but the allurements of worldly interests and pleasures. This is Israel's sin, to which they are lured by the women of Moab and Midian. Those are our worst enemies who lead us into sin, for that is the greatest harm anyone can do to us. Israel’s sin did what all of Balaam’s magic could not do; it turned God against them. Sicknesses are the results of God's anger, and just punishments for widespread sins; one infection follows another. Leaders in sin should be made examples of justice.

Phinehas, with the courage of zeal and faith, carried out vengeance on Zimri and Cozbi. This act cannot ever be an example for private revenge, or religious persecution, or for irregular public vengeance.

We do not read that any Midianites died from the plague; God punished them with the sword of an enemy, not with the rod of a father. We must oppose whatever is an opportunity for sin to us. Whatever leads us into sin should be distressing to us, like a thorn in the flesh. And none will be more surely and severely punished than those who, following Satan’s example, and with his cunning, tempt others to sin.
